clean_room: behavioral contracts only; no proprietary source/assets/branding; HIDE uses its own names and voice

entries.behavior: Esc hard-interrupts and cancels the in-flight tool while retaining all completed work in context; Ctrl+C is a second interrupt (clear input / exit).
entries.parity_requirement: Two independent gestures on a streaming turn: hard interrupt (cancel in-flight tool, keep prior results, return to prompt) and a second clear/exit control.
entries.enforcement: Must not discard tool results already returned; interrupt targets only the in-flight call.
entries.hide_evidence: FE SteerBar has cancel_run/pause; no live backend turn to interrupt (vertical slice broken)
entries.hawking_superiority: No network request to cancel -> interrupt is genuinely zero-latency; can fork a warm state at the interrupt point to branch both directions.

entries.behavior: Typing a correction + Enter is consumed as soon as the current tool completes, without stopping it.
entries.parity_requirement: Queue typed input to be injected at the next turn boundary without cancelling the running tool.
entries.hide_evidence: FE redirect_run custom intent exists; no live backend

entries.behavior: Ephemeral side-question mid-turn answered from in-context material only (no tools), in a dismissible overlay, never entering history, promotable to a real session.
entries.parity_requirement: A concurrent read-only side-channel query over current context that stays out of history and can be promoted.
entries.hawking_superiority: A warm fork answers on a second local decode stream at zero marginal cost and zero added latency to the main turn.

entries.behavior: A scriptable status line fed a stable JSON object (model, context used %, session cost+duration, cwd, git branch + staged/modified, PR number+state, rate-limit windows) whose stdout is rendered.
entries.parity_requirement: A status-line hook piping a documented JSON contract to a user shell script, updating live.
entries.hide_evidence: FE StatusBar real for phase/model/transport; branch + problems hardcoded
entries.hawking_superiority: No dollar meter -> show true local telemetry (tok/s, energy J/tok, resident GB, state-fork depth); exact context fill as a raw token/byte counter (not a percentage or budget bar, per the no-meter doctrine), read from local KV state.

entries.behavior: Agent-authored multi-step checklist with three-state markers, ~5 visible, persisting across compaction; shareable via a named ID.
entries.parity_requirement: A first-class task-list primitive the model writes to, a toggle, three-state rendering, windowed display, persistence across summarization.
entries.hide_evidence: FE PlanCard + todo rendering exist; no mock/live emits plan projection

entries.behavior: Tool/MCP calls collapse to one-liners by default; repeated same-server calls coalesce to a count; a transcript viewer expands raw I/O + per-message timestamp + model used.
entries.parity_requirement: Collapse-by-default tool rendering, count-coalescing, and a full-transcript toggle showing timestamps and per-message model.
entries.hide_evidence: FE tool chips + ChatPanel tool feed exist; data mock

entries.behavior: '/' opens one incremental fuzzy palette merging built-ins, skills, plugin commands, and MCP prompts; '@' picks files + MCP resources; '!' runs a shell command into context; ghost prompt suggestions from git history.
entries.parity_requirement: One merged incremental palette on '/', an '@' picker merging files and resources, a '!' shell affordance, dismissable ghost suggestions (suppressed after first turn / in plan mode).
entries.hide_evidence: FE CommandPalette (ui.tsx) real; MCP/skill unification absent (backend packed)

entries.behavior: Shift+Tab cycles default(Manual)/acceptEdits/plan (+optional bypassPermissions/auto) with an always-visible mode badge; also --permission-mode flag and permissions.defaultMode setting.
entries.parity_requirement: A single cycling chord over an ordered mode set, a permanent glanceable mode indicator, plus a CLI flag and settings key.
entries.hide_evidence: FE bypass-gate auto-approve exists; full mode cycle + enforcement need the packed kernel

entries.behavior: Plan mode allows reads + read-only shell but structurally blocks source edits until a written plan is approved via a graded dialog (auto / review-each / keep-refining / deeper-review); Ctrl+G edits the plan first.
entries.parity_requirement: Executor-level write block during planning (not prompt-based), a structured editable plan artifact, and a graded approval dialog that deterministically sets the post-approval mode.
entries.hide_evidence: FE PlanCard approve/edit/reorder intents exist; enforcement needs kernel + tool gate
entries.hawking_superiority: Best-of-N candidate plans executed speculatively in isolated local forks; user picks the plan that already worked, not a guess.

entries.behavior: allow/ask/deny arrays, deny->ask->allow precedence, first-match-wins; matchers: Bash(git commit:*) word-boundary globs with compound-command splitting, gitignore-style Read/Edit path scoping, WebFetch(domain:*), mcp__server__tool, Agent(Name); read-only Bash allowlist never prompts in any mode; symlink dual-path (allow needs both, deny needs either); protected paths (.git/.claude/rc) route to prompt before allow rules; rm -rf / circuit breaker even in bypass.
entries.parity_requirement: An ordered deny->ask->allow evaluator, shell-operator-aware compound decomposition, word-boundary globs, gitignore-style path scoping, protected-path pre-gate, and a non-defeatable destructive circuit breaker.
entries.hide_evidence: hide-security + hide-tools carry the pieces; hide-backend command-router gate; unwired
entries.hawking_superiority: Enforce at the OS/syscall boundary (Seatbelt) so a denied capability is physically absent; network default-off shrinks the deny race to an opt-in allowlist.

entries.behavior: read-only never prompts; a Bash 'don't ask again' persists permanently per-repo+command to settings.local.json; a file-edit approval persists only until session end.
entries.parity_requirement: Prompt offers allow-once / allow-for-session / allow-permanently (persisted, inspectable, per-repo) / deny, with persistence scope matched to risk.

entries.behavior: A separate classifier model gates every non-trivial action (sees user msgs + tool calls + rules but NOT raw tool-result content), auto-approves benign work, blocks escalation/exfiltration/destructive; falls back to prompting after repeated blocks; drops broad allow rules on entry.
entries.evidence: DOCUMENTED, MEASURED(~83% catch / 0.4% FP / 17% FN, Sonnet-4.6-class classifier)
entries.parity_requirement: An independent policy-evaluation pass per action, isolated from hostile tool-result content, with a documented block taxonomy and graceful fallback.
entries.hawking_superiority: Run the classifier as a warm local fork (no round-trip, no metered cost, no transcript egress); best-of-N safety judges reduce single-classifier error; egress-off default removes the exfiltration class entirely.

entries.behavior: On first open, project settings.json allow rules + additionalDirectories + MCP + hooks are parsed but INERT until the user accepts a trust dialog enumerating what would be granted; deny/ask always apply; trust keyed to git repo root. CVE-2026-33068 was exactly a case of resolving bypassPermissions before this gate.
entries.parity_requirement: A first-open trust gate that treats project config as data, lists exactly what a folder would grant, applies nothing capability-granting until accept, and resolves NO permission mode before the gate.
entries.hide_evidence: hide-security has audit/redaction/Seatbelt but no trust-before-config gate; serve binds 0.0.0.0 with no auth

entries.behavior: /sandbox: OS-enforced dual isolation (Seatbelt macOS, bubblewrap+seccomp Linux/WSL2), writes confined to cwd+temp inherited by every subprocess, reads = machine minus denies, network denied-by-default via an out-of-sandbox proxy with per-session domain approval + allow/deny domain lists; sandbox.credentials deny/mask; ~84% fewer prompts. Open-sourced as @anthropic-ai/sandbox-runtime (srt).
entries.parity_requirement: OS-primitive sandbox for every execution path, subprocess-inherited fs confinement, an out-of-sandbox egress proxy default-deny, credential deny/mask, and a one-command toggle that keeps deny rules + circuit breakers.
entries.hide_evidence: hide-security renders Seatbelt profiles + spawns sandbox-exec (pure logic real+tested); egress proxy / microVM are seams
entries.hawking_superiority: Local inference needs NO egress at all -> ship egress default-fully-off, air-gap as a hardware fact; snapshot the workspace to a capsule before a risky run for near-zero-cost rollback.

entries.behavior: CLAUDE.md at ./ and ./.claude/, walk up tree, root-first concat into a post-system user message; subdir CLAUDE.md loads lazily on touch; @path imports depth 4 (code-spans skipped, first-use approval); .claude/rules/*.md with paths: globs; claudeMdExcludes; CLAUDE.local.md; block-level HTML comments stripped. Instruction precedence Managed>User>Project>Local (read-last-wins).
entries.parity_requirement: Read an existing project's full CLAUDE.md tree + imports + rules verbatim with the exact precedence; migration must not require rewriting.
entries.hide_evidence: no CLAUDE.md reader in active tree; hawking-context memory store is packed
entries.hawking_superiority: Compile the resolved instruction set once into a warm-state capsule / prefix KV so instructions are free after the first fork, not re-tokenized each session.

entries.behavior: settings.json (user ~/.claude, project .claude, local .claude/settings.local.json, OS managed) with precedence Managed>CLI>Local>Project>User and permissions MERGING; managed-settings.d/*.json numeric-prefix ordering; managed claudeMd injection.
entries.parity_requirement: Four merge scopes with fixed precedence where deny always wins, a managed tier lower tiers can't override, and permissions merge (not override).

entries.behavior: Claude-authored memory at ~/.claude/projects/<repo-key>/memory/MEMORY.md (200 lines/25KB loaded), recalled next session; topic files linked from MEMORY.md.
entries.parity_requirement: Read an existing memory tree (MEMORY.md index + topic files, repo-derived key) at start; offer a quick-capture affordance (the '#' name is NOT current -> do not name-copy).
entries.hawking_superiority: Memory as a first-class warm-state capsule folded into the state fork -> no 200-line/25KB truncation cliff, no re-tokenization; deterministic offline dedup/merge.

entries.behavior: Every message/tool call/result streams to ~/.claude/projects/<sanitized-cwd>/<session-id>.jsonl instantly; survives exit/crash/reboot.
entries.parity_requirement: Append-per-turn transcript keyed by stable session ID under a predictable configurable root, written the instant each event happens.
entries.hide_evidence: hide-backend event bus + BackendReplayService exist (packed); FE store is event-sourced
entries.hawking_superiority: Persist the warm KV/state capsule alongside the text -> resume is instant with no re-prefill; unlimited full-fidelity local retention.

entries.behavior: --continue/-c (last in dir), --resume/-r (searchable picker: Space preview, Ctrl+W worktrees, Ctrl+A all projects, Ctrl+B branch, pasted PR URL), /resume, --from-pr; AI-generated titles; -n/--rename.
entries.parity_requirement: Continue-last, a searchable/nameable picker with scope widening and PR-URL resume, and AI titles.
entries.hide_evidence: FE Home recents + open_session; picker + scope widening absent

entries.behavior: Every prompt snapshots files (100 kept, 30-day cleanup); /rewind or empty Esc Esc restores code / conversation / both + Summarize from/up-to; persists across resume and /clear; /branch + --fork-session make a divergent copy. Does NOT cover bash-made or external file changes.
entries.parity_requirement: Pre-edit file snapshots + conversation checkpoints, a three-axis rewind menu, a bounded ring surviving resume, non-destructive /clear, and session forking.
entries.hide_evidence: FE StateTimeline scrub_to_event/fork_session intents; backend snapshot 'plan 2'
entries.hawking_superiority: Checkpoint the model KV/scratch state as a content-addressed capsule -> 'restore both' is atomic and instantaneous, unlimited depth, no 100-cap.

entries.behavior: Background sessions run detached under a per-user supervisor daemon (roster.json, jobs/<id>/state.json), survive sleep, restart from disk after reboot, monitored from a dashboard (claude agents), auto worktree commit/push/draft-PR (never main, never force-push).
entries.parity_requirement: A supervisor hosting detached sessions surviving terminal close + sleep, a one-screen dashboard grouped by state (working/needs-input/idle/done/failed) with peek/reply/attach, per-session worktree isolation, conditional auto-PR.
entries.hide_evidence: hide-fleet job DAG + isolation + merge (packed, not HTTP-reachable); FE FleetView optimistic
entries.hawking_superiority: No metered quota ceiling (Claude warns 10 parallel = 10x quota) -> parallelism bounded only by local hardware; state stays on-device.

entries.behavior: Specialists defined as .md + YAML frontmatter (name, description, tools, disallowedTools, model[inherit], skills, mcp, hooks, memory, permissions) in .claude/agents/ (project) + ~/.claude/agents/ (user); isolated context window; summary-only return; auto delegation via description or explicit @-mention / --agent; nesting depth 5; 200/session cap.
entries.parity_requirement: Load agent defs from project+user dirs with the same field semantics (name+description required; tools allowlist, disallowed applied first; model inherit default), isolated context, summary-only return, auto + explicit delegation, bounded nesting.
entries.hide_evidence: hide-kernel subagent support + hawking-orch role router (packed)
entries.hawking_superiority: Store each definition as a signed state capsule shipping its warmed prefix -> spawn is instant, not cold-start context assembly.

entries.behavior: A 'fork' worker inherits the ENTIRE conversation (system prompt, tools, model, history) and reuses the parent's prompt cache; forks run in background; fork-of-fork blocked.
entries.parity_requirement: A fork mode cloning the live conversation into a background worker sharing the parent's cache/prefix, returning only its result.
entries.hawking_superiority: Forking is the NATIVE Hawking primitive: a warm-state fork is a pointer-copy of a resident capsule -> unlimited cheap forks (best-of-N from one start), where Claude's fork advantage is only a prompt-cache hit.

entries.behavior: Experimental agent teams (CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1): a fixed lead spawns peer sessions sharing a task list (dependencies + file-locked claims) + mailboxes, messaging directly; ~7x tokens (teammates in plan mode); one team/session; no nested teams.
entries.parity_requirement: A lead + peer sessions with a shared dependency-gated race-safe task list, per-agent mailboxes with push delivery, direct addressing, plus honest cost/fit guidance.
entries.hawking_superiority: Resident shared-memory forks collapse the ~7x token explosion toward the cost of one -> teams become a default, not a rationed premium.

entries.behavior: /goal: a session-scoped loop where after each turn a separate fast model (default Haiku) judges a user completion condition (<=4000 chars) against TRANSCRIPT EVIDENCE ONLY (never runs tools), feeding a reason back on 'not yet'; requires trust; pairs with auto mode for unattended runs.
entries.parity_requirement: A completion loop with a fresh transcript-only evaluator separate from the worker, reason-feedback on miss, and objective stop.
entries.hawking_superiority: Run the evaluator as a local Haiku-class fork at zero marginal cost -> evaluate more often (even mid-turn) and best-of-N judges to reduce single-evaluator error.

entries.behavior: SKILL.md (YAML frontmatter + body) + optional bundled scripts; only name+description in context, body loads on trigger (auto description-match or /skill-name), bundled files load on reference; allowed-tools scoped to the invoking turn; context:fork+agent; model/effort; !`cmd` dynamic injection; scope enterprise>personal>project; plugin:skill namespacing; custom commands merged into skills.
entries.parity_requirement: A two-tier registry (cheap name+description listing + lazy body load), description-matching auto-trigger with opt-out, a skill-dir contract with a dir path var, turn-scoped tool pre-approval, and context:fork.
entries.hide_evidence: FE ContextStack SKILLS is a hardcoded const; no skill runtime
entries.hawking_superiority: Pre-index the skill corpus into a resident capsule; hydrate a body by forking it -> O(1) load, no per-turn token accounting, no eviction; bundled scripts touch .tq artifacts / GPU / private data a cloud sandbox can't.

entries.behavior: .claude-plugin/plugin.json manifest bundling skills/agents/hooks/.mcp.json/.lsp.json/monitors/themes/output-styles/settings.json + userConfig(sensitive); installed via /plugin from marketplaces (marketplace.json, git/owner-repo/URL/local); SHA-pinned; 'Will install' inventory + per-turn context-cost estimate; unused-plugin detection; ~11 LSP language plugins; auto-update.
entries.parity_requirement: A manifest-defined bundle with convention dirs per component type, install/enable/disable/uninstall lifecycle, namespaced names, marketplace add-then-install, SHA pinning, pre-install inventory + cost estimate, admin allowlists.
entries.hawking_superiority: Bundles can ship local runtime assets a cloud plugin can't: quantized model shards, Metal kernels, state-capsule presets -> install an entire specialized local model+workflow.

entries.behavior: Hooks fire at PreToolUse/PostToolUse/UserPromptSubmit/Stop/SessionStart/PreCompact/Notification/SubagentStop/PermissionRequest/ConfigChange/InstructionsLoaded with matchers; stdin-JSON + exit-code + JSON-decision (allow/deny/ask, block+reason, updatedInput, additionalContext, updatedToolOutput). Hooks execute only after trust.
entries.parity_requirement: The same event taxonomy with matchers, JSON-on-stdin, exit-code semantics, and a JSON decision object; PreToolUse as a hard pre-execution gate that can deny/ask/allow/rewrite; NO project hook runs before trust.
entries.hide_evidence: no hook lifecycle engine is packed; hide-backend command-router is adjacent permission-routing, not a hook engine
entries.hawking_superiority: Local hooks are latency-free and can read/modify true runtime state (live KV, resident model) not a serialized transcript; enforce the gate at the syscall/sandbox layer.

entries.behavior: .mcp.json (project) + ~/.claude.json (user/local) + managed; stdio/HTTP(streamable-http)/SSE(deprecated)/WebSocket; OAuth 2.0 (DCR/CIMD/pinned scopes); @server:proto://path resources; /mcp__server__prompt prompts; tool-search deferral default-on; output warn 10k/hard 25k spill-to-disk; elicitation; claude mcp add/list/get/remove/login.
entries.parity_requirement: Be MCP client AND host AND a server exposing HIDE's own tools/agents; read a repo-root manifest layered under local/user/managed; support all live transports + OAuth; @-resource mentions; prompts as slash commands; deferred tool loading; bounded output with disk spill.
entries.hide_evidence: hide-tools has a JSON-RPC MCP client (stdio + Streamable HTTP), packed; no host/server
entries.hawking_superiority: Keep long-lived local stdio servers warm across sessions; snapshot the resolved+authed server set into a capsule so a fork inherits connected servers instantly.

entries.behavior: TS + Python SDKs = same engine; claude -p with --output-format text/json/stream-json, --json-schema (result in structured_output field), --allowedTools/--disallowedTools, --permission-mode, --max-turns, --mcp-config, session resume/fork; result carries total_cost_usd/tokens/num_turns/duration_ms/session_id; --bare hermetic mode (no auto-discovery; becoming -p default); settingSources controls whether .claude/CLAUDE.md travel.
entries.parity_requirement: An embeddable local Agent SDK (>=2 langs) with a query()-style API + options parity, plus a headless -p with structured output, tool allow/deny, permission modes, max-turns, MCP config, session resume/fork, a result object, and a hermetic --bare mode.
entries.hawking_superiority: forkSession is an O(1) copy-on-write of live model+conversation state (warm-state fork), not a JSONL re-read; return a truthful $0.00 / joules; unlimited best-of-N with no rate limit; byte-reproducible runs (fixed seed + resident weights).

entries.behavior: Native VS Code/Cursor extension: editable plan doc + inline comments, side-by-side accept/reject diffs, @file#L-ranges, checkpoints, parallel tabs, cloud Remote tab; a loopback 'ide' MCP server (lock file 0600, per-activation token) sharing active selection + open-file + LSP diagnostics into the session. JetBrains plugin (beta, terminal-based, only getDiagnostics). CLI and IDE share ONE conversation history + checkpoints.
entries.parity_requirement: One session across a terminal-style Chat and an editor IDE: a loopback token-authed bridge injecting selection+file+diagnostics, native diff-with-permission review, editable plan doc, shared history + checkpoints across both surfaces.
entries.hide_evidence: FE has both Chat and Code chambers sharing one Zustand store + wire; backend bridge packed
entries.hawking_superiority: Local engine -> zero network round-trip for diffs/selection/diagnostics; both surfaces share ONE warm resident context instead of re-injecting selection per prompt.

entries.behavior: /usage (merged /cost, /stats) shows session totals + per-model + per-skill/subagent/plugin/MCP attribution, plan bars, 5-hour + weekly caps; /context grid; /compact [focus]. Pain: /usage is local-session-only.
entries.parity_requirement: An at-any-time usage/telemetry view with attribution, a context-window map with optimization hints, and a focus-able compaction command.
entries.hide_evidence: FE Digest + ContextStack exist; doctrine already forbids a budget/cap meter
entries.hawking_superiority: STRONGEST INVERSION: no meter, no plan limits -> replace budget bars with pure performance telemetry (tok/s, energy, parallelism) framed as capability headroom, not a countdown; compaction can be reversible detail-hiding, not lossy summarization.
